title: Custom Starboards
description:
## Summary

Add configurable, plural, per-server "starboards" to Angela Lansbury 9000. Server admins use `/starboard add` to designate a destination channel, a trigger emoji, and a reaction threshold. When any message outside that destination earns the threshold count of that emoji, Angela reposts it (as an embed with attribution, content, media link, jump link, and a live count) into the destination channel. A guild may configure any number of starboards; a single emoji may feed several. Admins can `list`, `edit`, and `remove` boards. Counts stay current as reactions change; deleting the original removes the repost.

Secondary deliverable: the repo's first automated test suite (pytest + pytest-asyncio), with fixtures making the existing DB and Discord objects testable.

## Phases (child issues)

- Phase 0: Test harness foundation
- Phase 1: Persistence + matching
- Phase 2: Event handlers
- Phase 3: Command cog

Phases are sequential: P0 → P1 → P2 → P3.

## Decisions locked

1. Plural starboards per guild (surrogate `id` PK; `guild_id` indexed, non-unique).
2. Emoji fan-out: one emoji may drive multiple boards; no `(guild_id, emoji)` uniqueness.
3. Self-stars and bot reactions both count; never call `reaction.users()`.
4. Cache-first counting via `bot.cached_messages`; `fetch_message` only on cache miss.
5. Post-once then edit; removal edits, never deletes.
6. Link media, do not re-host (broken links after deletion are intentional/privacy).
7. Honour original deletion: delete starboard post(s) + clear entry on `on_raw_message_delete`.
8. In-memory config cache per guild, mirroring `activity_module_settings_helper`; invalidate on mutation.
9. Global rollout (`force_global`); no per-guild gating.
10. Emoji input = string SlashOption + parse (unicode and `<:name:id>`).
11. Eligible sources = all readable channels except each board's own target (ignore-list/NSFW deferred).
12. Tests in-scope; every phase ships with them.

## References

- Plan: `.docs/plans/2026-06-22-custom-starboards.md`
- Plan (visual): `.docs/plans/2026-06-22-custom-starboards.html`
- Research: `.docs/research/2026-06-22-custom-starboards.html`

title: Phase 0: Test harness foundation
description:
## Summary

Stand up the repository's first automated test suite (pytest + pytest-asyncio) before any feature code, so every later phase ships with tests. Make the hardcoded DB singleton testable by sourcing its URL from an environment variable, and provide fixtures for a throwaway DB (with per-test rollback) and for faking Discord objects without a live connection.

## Files

- MODIFY `Pipfile` — add `pytest` and `pytest-asyncio` to `[dev-packages]`.
- MODIFY `db/model/__init__.py` (currently line 3: `DB = SQLAlchemy('sqlite:///bumper-db.sqlite')`) — make the URL env-configurable.
- NEW `tests/__init__.py`
- NEW `tests/conftest.py` — fixtures: temp DB schema, per-test rollback, fake Discord object factories.
- NEW `tests/test_harness_smoke.py` — proves the harness runs.
- NEW `pytest.ini` (or `[tool.pytest.ini_options]` in a config file) — set `asyncio_mode = auto`.

## Implementation Details

The DB is a hardcoded singleton at `db/model/__init__.py:3`. Tests must not touch the real `bumper-db.sqlite`. Make the URL env-driven while preserving prod behaviour:

```python
# db/model/__init__.py
import os
from sqla_wrapper import SQLAlchemy

DB = SQLAlchemy(os.environ.get('DATABASE_URL', 'sqlite:///bumper-db.sqlite'))
```

`tests/conftest.py` must set `DATABASE_URL` to a temp sqlite file BEFORE importing `db` (pytest imports conftest before collecting test modules, so module-level env setup here runs first). `sqla_wrapper` provides the needed primitives (verified against the installed lib): `DB.create_all()`, `DB.drop_all()`, and `DB.test_transaction(savepoint=True)` (rolls back everything at the end).

```python
# tests/conftest.py
import os, tempfile, pytest
os.environ.setdefault('DATABASE_URL', f'sqlite:///{tempfile.mkstemp(suffix=".sqlite")[1]}')

import db                       # noqa: imports DB + every model via db/__init__.py
from db import DB

@pytest.fixture(scope='session', autouse=True)
def _schema():
    DB.create_all()
    yield
    DB.drop_all()

@pytest.fixture(autouse=True)
def db_tx():
    tx = DB.test_transaction(savepoint=True)
    yield
    tx.close()
```

Add minimal Discord fake factories in conftest (plain objects or `unittest.mock.MagicMock`), exposing only the attributes the production code reads:

- `make_payload(emoji=..., channel_id=..., message_id=..., guild_id=..., member=...)` — shaped like `nextcord.RawReactionActionEvent` (attrs: `guild_id`, `channel_id`, `message_id`, `user_id`, `emoji`, `member`, `event_type`).
- `make_emoji(name, id=None, animated=False)` — shaped like `nextcord.PartialEmoji`; `str()` must render the unicode char when `id is None`, else `<:name:id>` / `<a:name:id>`.
- `make_message(reactions=[...], content=..., attachments=[...], author=...)` — `reactions` are objects with `.emoji` and `.count`.

These factories are reused by P1/P2/P3 tests.

## Gotchas & Context

- **Import order is the critical risk.** If any module imports `db` before conftest sets `DATABASE_URL`, the engine binds to the prod file. Keep the `os.environ.setdefault('DATABASE_URL', ...)` line at the very top of `tests/conftest.py`, above `import db`.
- `db/__init__.py` already imports every model module; the session-scoped `DB.create_all()` will build the whole schema (including future starboard tables once P1 registers them).
- The project uses `pipenv`. Dev deps are installed with `pipenv install --dev`.
- nextcord `PartialEmoji`: `is_custom_emoji()` is `id is not None`; `__str__` renders unicode vs `<:name:id>` (nextcord/partial_emoji.py:160-192).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `pipenv install --dev` installs pytest + pytest-asyncio; they appear in `Pipfile`/`Pipfile.lock`.
- [ ] `db/model/__init__.py` reads `DATABASE_URL` with the prod sqlite path as default (prod behaviour unchanged when the env var is unset).
- [ ] `pipenv run pytest` runs green.
- [ ] `tests/test_harness_smoke.py` asserts: (a) the active DB URL is NOT the prod `bumper-db.sqlite`, (b) a trivial model insert + query works inside the rollback transaction, (c) an `async def` test executes (proving `asyncio_mode = auto`).
- [ ] No test run writes to the real `bumper-db.sqlite`.

## References

- Plan: `.docs/plans/2026-06-22-custom-starboards.md` (Phase 0)
- Research: `.docs/research/2026-06-22-custom-starboards.html`
- Epic: angela-lansbury-9000-17p

title: Phase 1: Persistence + matching
description:
## Summary

Add the two database tables (`starboard_config`, `starboard_entry`), an Alembic migration, and the cached CRUD helper plus the canonical `emoji_matches` function reused by the handler and the cog. This is the persistence + matching foundation for all later phases.

## Files

- NEW `db/model/starboard_config.py`
- NEW `db/model/starboard_entry.py`
- MODIFY `db/__init__.py` — add `from db.model.starboard_config import *` and `from db.model.starboard_entry import *` (it explicitly lists every model so Alembic + create_all see them; see lines 8-19). Re-export `StarboardConfig`/`StarboardEntry` so `from db import StarboardConfig, StarboardEntry` works, mirroring how `AutoDeleteChannelConfig` is exposed.
- NEW `db/migrations/<date>_<hash>_add_starboard_tables.py` (autogenerated)
- NEW `db/helpers/starboard_helper.py` — CRUD + per-guild cache + `emoji_matches`.
- NEW `tests/test_starboard_helper.py`

## Implementation Details

### Models (SQLAlchemy 2.0 `Mapped`/`mapped_column`, matching existing models)

```python
# db/model/starboard_config.py
class StarboardConfig(DB.Model):
    __tablename__ = 'starboard_config'
    id: Mapped[int] = mapped_column(primary_key=True)
    guild_id: Mapped[int] = mapped_column(index=True)
    target_channel_id: Mapped[int] = mapped_column()
    emoji: Mapped[str] = mapped_column()                  # unicode char OR custom name
    emoji_id: Mapped[int] = mapped_column(nullable=True)  # set only for custom emoji
    threshold: Mapped[int] = mapped_column(default=5)
    enabled: Mapped[bool] = mapped_column(default=True)
    name: Mapped[str] = mapped_column(nullable=True)

# db/model/starboard_entry.py
class StarboardEntry(DB.Model):
    __tablename__ = 'starboard_entry'
    id: Mapped[int] = mapped_column(primary_key=True)
    starboard_config_id: Mapped[int] = mapped_column(ForeignKey('starboard_config.id'), index=True)
    guild_id: Mapped[int] = mapped_column(index=True)
    original_message_id: Mapped[int] = mapped_column(index=True)
    original_channel_id: Mapped[int] = mapped_column()
    posted_message_id: Mapped[int] = mapped_column(nullable=True)
    author_id: Mapped[int] = mapped_column()
    star_count: Mapped[int] = mapped_column(default=0)
    __table_args__ = (UniqueConstraint('starboard_config_id', 'original_message_id'),)
```

Unique constraint on `(starboard_config_id, original_message_id)` makes each board post a given message at most once and makes concurrent inserts collide deterministically. NO uniqueness on `(guild_id, emoji)` — fan-out is intentional.

### Helper (`db/helpers/starboard_helper.py`)

Follow the in-memory cache pattern in `db/helpers/activity_module_settings_helper.py` (module-level dict keyed by `guild_id`). Use the `DB.s` API (`DB.s.first`, `DB.s.all`, `DB.s.query`, `DB.s.add`, `DB.s.delete`, `DB.s.commit`). API surface:

- `get_enabled_configs(guild_id) -> list[StarboardConfig]` — cached; hot path for the reaction handler.
- `get_configs(guild_id)`, `get_config(config_id)`, `add_config(...)`, `update_config(config_id, **kw)`, `remove_config(config_id)` — every mutator INVALIDATES the guild's cache entry.
- `get_entry(config_id, message_id)`, `upsert_entry(...)`, `delete_entries_for_message(guild_id, message_id) -> list[StarboardEntry]` (returns removed rows so the delete handler in P2 knows which posts to remove).
- `emoji_matches(payload_emoji, config) -> bool` — CANONICAL matcher: when `config.emoji_id` is set, match `payload_emoji.id == config.emoji_id`; else match `str(payload_emoji) == config.emoji`. (Unicode emoji have `id is None`; custom should be matched by id since names can be reused.)

### Migration

Generate with `pipenv run python cli.py db revision "add starboard tables"` (proper-cli autogenerate compares models to DB). Verify `upgrade()` creates both tables, the indexes, and the unique constraint; then `pipenv run python cli.py db upgrade`. Migration files follow `YYYY_MM_DD_<hash>_<desc>.py` (template: db/migrations/2024_03_19_c7a45ca9ec78_added_poll_models.py).

## Gotchas & Context

- **Model registration:** if `db/__init__.py` does not import the new model modules, `create_all`/autogenerate silently skip the tables AND the P0 test schema won't include them. This is the most common failure here.
- `DB.s.first(Model, **attrs)` returns one row or None; `DB.s.all(Model, **attrs)` returns a list — used throughout existing helpers.
- Duplicate-insert handling: catch `sqlalchemy.exc.IntegrityError` + `DB.s.rollback()` (pattern in db/helpers/birthday_helper.py:14-21).
- The cache pattern to copy: `db/helpers/activity_module_settings_helper.py` (module-level `__CACHE` dict; populate on read, drop the guild key on mutation).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Both models exist, are imported in `db/__init__.py`, and re-exported from the `db` package.
- [ ] A new Alembic migration creates both tables + indexes + the unique constraint; `pipenv run python cli.py db upgrade` applies cleanly.
- [ ] Tests: insert/read a config; multiple configs per guild; multiple configs sharing one emoji (fan-out) all persist.
- [ ] Tests: `emoji_matches` — unicode match and non-match; custom match by id even when names differ; custom-vs-unicode mismatch.
- [ ] Tests: `get_enabled_configs` returns only enabled rows; a mutator invalidates the cache so the next read reflects the change.
- [ ] Tests: `upsert_entry` is idempotent on `(config, message)`; a duplicate insert raises `IntegrityError` and the rollback path is exercised; `delete_entries_for_message` returns all matching rows across boards.
- [ ] `pipenv run pytest` green; no test touches the real DB.

## References

- Plan: `.docs/plans/2026-06-22-custom-starboards.md` (Phase 1)
- Research: `.docs/research/2026-06-22-custom-starboards.html`
- Epic: angela-lansbury-9000-17p; depends on Phase 0 (angela-lansbury-9000-17p.1)

comments.author: coordinator
comments.text:
Implementation complete: Phase 1 (Persistence + matching) complete for custom starboards.

NEW db/model/starboard_config.py + starboard_entry.py (SQLAlchemy 2.0 Mapped/mapped_column; StarboardEntry has UniqueConstraint('starboard_config_id','original_message_id'); no (guild_id,emoji) uniqueness — fan-out intentional). MODIFIED db/__init__.py to import + re-export both models (verified `from db import StarboardConfig, StarboardEntry` works).

Migration: stamped the empty dev DB at head c7a45ca9ec78 (it had never been alembic-managed), autogenerated + hand-trimmed db/migrations/2026_06_23_44433ddac1b1_add_starboard_tables.py to contain ONLY the starboard tables (matching the poll-migration convention). `cli.py db upgrade` applied cleanly — both tables, all 4 indexes, and the unique constraint verified in sqlite_master; alembic_version now 44433ddac1b1.

NEW db/helpers/starboard_helper.py: per-guild in-memory cache (mirrors activity_module_settings_helper) with get_enabled_configs (cached hot path); get_configs/get_config/add_config/update_config/remove_config (every mutator invalidates the guild cache); get_entry/upsert_entry (idempotent, catches IntegrityError + DB.s.rollback per birthday_helper pattern)/delete_entries_for_message (returns removed rows); canonical emoji_matches (by emoji_id when set, else str(payload)==config.emoji). Uses DB.s API.

NEW tests/test_starboard_helper.py: 19 tests reusing conftest's make_emoji factory — config persistence/fan-out, emoji_matches (unicode/custom-by-id/mismatch), get_enabled_configs enabled-only + real cache hit + invalidation on every mutator, upsert idempotency, duplicate-insert IntegrityError+rollback, upsert internal race-recovery branch, delete across multiple boards. An autouse fixture clears the module-level cache between tests (it survives the per-test DB rollback).

Tests: 24 passing (5 prior harness + 19 new). Acceptance: 7/7 criteria met with evidence. No dead code / test-only helpers / swallowed errors. Real bumper-db.sqlite is gitignored and untouched by the test run (tests use the temp DATABASE_URL). NOT committed — awaiting review approval per coordinator instructions.

Files: db/model/starboard_config.py, db/model/starboard_entry.py, db/__init__.py, db/migrations/2026_06_23_44433ddac1b1_add_starboard_tables.py, db/helpers/starboard_helper.py, tests/test_starboard_helper.py.

title: Phase 2: Event handlers
description:
## Summary

Implement all starboard gateway-event handling: reaction add/remove (cache-first counting, fan-out, post-once-then-edit) and original-message-delete cleanup, plus the pure `reaction_count` helper and the `starboard_embed` builder these need. Extend the existing reaction event closures rather than registering new ones.

## Files

- NEW `bot/cogs/starboard/__init__.py`
- NEW `bot/cogs/starboard/starboard_utils.py` — `reaction_count(message, config)` (pure).
- MODIFY `bot/utils/messages.py` — add `starboard_embed(...)`.
- NEW `bot/events/handlers/starboard_handler.py` — `handle_reaction_add`, `handle_reaction_remove`, `handle_message_delete`, `post_or_edit`.
- MODIFY `bot/events/on_raw_reaction_add_event.py` — call the starboard handler alongside `temp_discussion_handler` (do NOT register a second `on_raw_reaction_add`).
- MODIFY `bot/events/on_raw_reaction_remove_event.py` — same.
- NEW `bot/events/on_raw_message_delete_event.py`
- MODIFY `bot/app.py` — register the message-delete event in `run()` (alongside the existing reaction event registrations at lines 72-73).
- NEW `tests/test_starboard_handler.py`, `tests/test_starboard_utils.py`

## Implementation Details

### Pure helpers (tested in isolation, no mocks)

- `reaction_count(message, config) -> int`: find the matching `Reaction` in `message.reactions` (reuse `starboard_helper.emoji_matches`) and return `.count`, else `0`.
- `messages.starboard_embed(message, emoji_display, count, source_channel) -> nextcord.Embed`: `set_author(name=message.author.display_name, icon_url=message.author.display_avatar.url)`; `description = message.content`; if the message has an image attachment, `set_image(url=<first image attachment .url>)` (reuse `message_has_image`, bot/utils/messages.py:11-16); add a footer/field `{emoji_display} {count} · #{source_channel}`; `timestamp = message.created_at`; include `message.jump_url`. An embed inlines only one image; list extra/non-image attachments as links.

### Reaction handler

Extend the existing closure (keeps `temp_discussion_handler` working):

```python
# bot/events/on_raw_reaction_add_event.py
def register_event(bot):
    @bot.event
    async def on_raw_reaction_add(payload):
        await temp_discussion_handler.handle_closure_react(bot, payload)
        await starboard_handler.handle_reaction_add(bot, payload)
```

- `handle_reaction_add(bot, payload)`: return if `payload.guild_id is None`; `configs = starboard_helper.get_enabled_configs(payload.guild_id)`; `matches = [c for c in configs if starboard_helper.emoji_matches(payload.emoji, c) and payload.channel_id != c.target_channel_id]`; return early if none (the common case — no I/O); resolve the message CACHE-FIRST via `nextcord.utils.get(bot.cached_messages, id=payload.message_id)`, falling back to `bot_utils.get_or_fetch_channel(bot, payload.channel_id)` + `channel.fetch_message(...)` (handle None / `nextcord.NotFound`); for each match compute `reaction_count(message, c)` and call `post_or_edit`.
- `post_or_edit(bot, config, message, count, source_channel)`: `entry = starboard_helper.get_entry(config.id, message.id)`. If `entry is None` and `count >= config.threshold`: build the embed, `send` to the target channel (`get_or_fetch_channel`), then `upsert_entry` with `posted_message_id` + `star_count`. If `entry` exists: fetch `entry.posted_message_id` in the target channel and `edit` its embed to refresh the count (and update `star_count`). Wrap sends/edits in `try/except (nextcord.Forbidden, nextcord.NotFound)` → Sentry, matching the auto-delete error handling (bot/cogs/auto_delete/auto_delete_commands.py:53-71).
- `handle_reaction_remove(bot, payload)`: same matching; if an entry exists, recompute count and edit the post. NEVER delete on un-react.

### Delete propagation

`bot/events/on_raw_message_delete_event.py` registers `on_raw_message_delete(payload)` → `starboard_handler.handle_message_delete(bot, payload)`. The handler: `entries = starboard_helper.delete_entries_for_message(payload.guild_id, payload.message_id)`; for each entry resolve the target channel and delete `posted_message_id` (tolerate `NotFound`/`Forbidden`). `nextcord.RawMessageDeleteEvent` carries `message_id`, `channel_id`, `guild_id` and fires for uncached messages too. No new intents needed (`messages` + `guilds` already enabled in bot/app.py:18).

## Gotchas & Context

- **Handler chaining is the #1 gotcha:** `@bot.event` keeps only ONE callback per event name. Adding a second `@bot.event async def on_raw_reaction_add` would silently clobber `temp_discussion_handler`. The starboard call MUST be added inside the existing closures. Wrap the starboard call (try/except → Sentry) so an exception there doesn't swallow the closure handler and vice versa.
- **No count in the payload:** `RawReactionActionEvent` has no reaction count — that's why we read it from the (cached) `message.reactions`.
- **Cache-first matters:** nextcord keeps `Reaction.count` live in memory for cached messages (max_messages default 1000), so cache hits cost zero REST calls. Only `fetch_message` on a cache miss. A 404 on a deleted message is safe (does not count toward Discord's invalid-request ban).
- `payload.member` is populated on ADD events only; remove events have no member.
- Fetch helpers exist: `bot/utils/bot_utils.py` (`get_or_fetch_guild`, `get_or_fetch_channel`).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Pure-helper tests: `reaction_count` returns the right count, `0` when the emoji is absent, and the correct value among multiple reactions; `starboard_embed` includes author/description/jump-url/count, sets an image only when an image attachment exists, and handles empty content (media-only message).
- [ ] Handler tests (fake bot/message/payload, no network): below threshold → no entry, no send; crossing threshold → exactly one send and an entry with `posted_message_id`; an already-posted message getting another reaction → edit path, no second send; a reaction inside the board's own target channel → skipped; fan-out → a message matching two boards posts to both targets with two entries; cache hit does NOT call `fetch_message` (assert the mock is uncalled) while a cache miss does; self-star / bot reaction still counts; remove below threshold edits (not deletes) the post.
- [ ] Delete tests: deleting an original deletes the posted message(s) and clears entries across all boards it fed; deleting a message with no entry is a no-op; a `NotFound` while deleting the post is swallowed and the entry is still cleared.
- [ ] `bot/app.py` `run()` registers the new message-delete event; both reaction events call the starboard handler in addition to `temp_discussion_handler`.
- [ ] `pipenv run pytest` green; no test touches the real DB.

## References

- Plan: `.docs/plans/2026-06-22-custom-starboards.md` (Phase 2)
- Research: `.docs/research/2026-06-22-custom-starboards.html`
- Epic: angela-lansbury-9000-17p; depends on Phase 1 (angela-lansbury-9000-17p.2)

comments.author: coordinator
comments.text:
Implementation complete: Phase 2 (Event handlers) complete for custom starboards. NEW: bot/cogs/starboard/{__init__,starboard_utils}.py (pure reaction_count via emoji_matches); bot/events/handlers/starboard_handler.py (handle_reaction_add/remove, handle_message_delete, post_or_edit — cache-first message resolution via bot.cached_messages, fetch_message only on miss; post-once-then-edit; remove edits never deletes; Forbidden/NotFound→Sentry); bot/events/on_raw_message_delete_event.py; tests/test_starboard_handler.py (16 tests) + tests/test_starboard_utils.py (10 tests). MODIFIED: bot/utils/messages.py (starboard_embed — author/desc/image-only-if-image-attachment via message_has_image/extra attachment links/jump field/emoji-count-channel footer/timestamp); both reaction event closures chain the starboard handler ALONGSIDE temp_discussion_handler (no second @bot.event registration — each call try/except→Sentry isolated); bot/app.py run() registers the message-delete event.

Tests: 53 passing (whole suite, pipenv run pytest); tests use a temp DATABASE_URL sqlite — real bumper-db.sqlite untouched. Acceptance: all criteria met — below/at/above threshold; edit path with no second send; reaction in target channel skipped; fan-out to two boards (two entries); cache hit asserts fetch_message uncalled vs miss calls it; self-star counts (no reaction.users() anywhere); remove edits not deletes; delete propagation across boards; NotFound swallowed + entry still cleared; no-guild/no-entry no-ops. No dead code (every new fn called from production, grep-verified), no test-only helpers, no swallowed errors. Not committed per instructions — awaiting your review.

title: Phase 3: Command cog
description:
## Summary

Add the admin-facing `/starboard` command cog (`add|list|edit|remove`), the pure `parse_emoji_input` helper it relies on, the cog/event registrations, and the README "Running tests" section. After this phase the feature is fully wired and shippable.

## Files

- MODIFY `bot/cogs/starboard/starboard_utils.py` — add `parse_emoji_input(raw)`.
- NEW `bot/cogs/starboard/starboard_commands.py` — `StarboardCommands(commands.Cog)`.
- MODIFY `bot/app.py` — `bot.add_cog(StarboardCommands(bot))` in `run()` (alongside the other `add_cog` calls, lines 61-68).
- MODIFY `bot/cogs/__init__.py` — export `StarboardCommands` if following the existing pattern.
- MODIFY `README.md` — add a "Running tests" section (`pipenv install --dev`, `pipenv run pytest`, and the `DATABASE_URL` override note).
- MODIFY `Pipfile.lock` — regenerated by `pipenv install --dev`.
- NEW `tests/test_starboard_commands.py`

## Implementation Details

### Emoji parser (pure)

`parse_emoji_input(raw: str) -> tuple[str, int | None]`: regex `<a?:(\w+):(\d+)>` → `(name, int(id))` for custom emoji; otherwise treat as unicode → `(raw.strip(), None)`. Raise `ValueError` on empty/garbage input. Lives alongside `reaction_count` in `bot/cogs/starboard/starboard_utils.py`.

### Command cog

Root group gated with `default_member_permissions=Permissions(manage_guild=True)` and `force_global=True` (mirrors bot/cogs/server_admin/server_admin_commands.py:10-15). Subcommands:

- `add(channel: nextcord.TextChannel, emoji: str, threshold: int, name: str = optional)`: `parse_emoji_input(emoji)`; if a custom id was parsed, validate it belongs to the guild via `nextcord.utils.get(interaction.guild.emojis, id=...)` and reject foreign emoji; preflight `channel.permissions_for(interaction.guild.me)` for `send_messages` + `embed_links` (pattern: auto_delete_commands.py:144-147) and warn if missing; `starboard_helper.add_config(...)` (which invalidates the cache); confirm with `messages.success(...)`.
- `list()`: build an embed of the guild's boards (target · emoji · threshold · enabled · name); paginate only if needed (pattern options: bot/cogs/server_admin/check_perms_view.py ListPageSource or the hand-rolled view in image_message_delete).
- `edit(starboard: <autocomplete>, threshold?, enabled?, channel?, emoji?)`: update only provided fields; invalidate cache.
- `remove(starboard: <autocomplete, value=id>)`: `starboard_helper.remove_config(id)` (deletes config + its entries); invalidate cache.

Use typed `SlashOption`s for channel/threshold. For emoji, take a string then `parse_emoji_input`; on `ValueError` reply `messages.error(...)` ephemerally. Autocomplete for `remove`/`edit` via `@cmd.on_autocomplete('starboard')` → `interaction.response.send_autocomplete({label: str(id)})` (pattern: bot/cogs/polls/poll_commands.py:52-60); label = `name` or `#channel · emoji ·≥N`.

Confirm `run()` registers the cog and all event modules (the two reaction events already wired in P2 + the new message-delete event).

## Gotchas & Context

- No existing command takes an emoji input — `parse_emoji_input` is new ground; cover unicode, custom, animated custom, and garbage.
- `default_member_permissions=Permissions(manage_guild=True)` is the project's admin-gating mechanism; `force_global=True` ships globally (no `guild_ids` gate).
- Cache invalidation on every mutation (`add`/`edit`/`remove`) is required so the P2 hot path sees changes.
- Full interaction round-trips are hard to unit-test; extract and test the underlying logic (emoji validation against guild, the label/display builder, and the helper CRUD paths).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `/starboard add|list|edit|remove` exist, are restricted to `manage_guild`, and are registered globally.
- [ ] `remove`/`edit` offer autocomplete over the guild's boards.
- [ ] Custom and unicode emoji both work; foreign/garbage emoji are rejected with a clear ephemeral error.
- [ ] Each mutation invalidates the per-guild config cache.
- [ ] Tests: `parse_emoji_input` (unicode 📖 → `('📖', None)`; `<:book:123>` → `('book', 123)`; animated `<a:book:123>`; empty/garbage → `ValueError`); emoji-validation-against-guild; label/display builder; create/update/remove paths through `starboard_helper` assert DB state + cache invalidation.
- [ ] README documents `pipenv install --dev`, `pipenv run pytest`, and the `DATABASE_URL` override.
- [ ] Manual smoke test in the test guild passes: add a board, react past threshold in another channel, confirm the repost, add a reaction (count edits), remove reactions (post stays), delete the original (post disappears).
- [ ] Whole suite green: `pipenv run pytest`; no test touches the real `bumper-db.sqlite`.

## References

- Plan: `.docs/plans/2026-06-22-custom-starboards.md` (Phase 3)
- Research: `.docs/research/2026-06-22-custom-starboards.html`
- Epic: angela-lansbury-9000-17p; depends on Phase 1 (angela-lansbury-9000-17p.2) and Phase 2 (angela-lansbury-9000-17p.3)

title: Phase 6: SQLite to Postgres data migration script
description:
## Summary
Write a one-off script that copies all data from the old `bumper-db.sqlite` into the new Postgres schema, transforming the two cleaned-up structures (CSV -> junction rows; composite PK -> surrogate id) during the copy.

## Files
- N `scripts/migrate_sqlite_to_postgres.py`

## Implementation Details
- Open TWO engines: a read-only connection to the old `bumper-db.sqlite`, and the new Postgres engine (via `DATABASE_URL`).
- Copy table by table in FK-safe order (parents before children), writing through the corrected ORM models so values land in `BigInteger` columns:
  - `starboard_config` before `starboard_entry`.
  - `poll_question` -> `poll_choice` -> `poll_response`.
  - Standalone tables (guild_config, user_settings, etc.) any time.
- Transform during copy:
  - For each `activity_module_settings` row, split the old `excluded_channels` CSV string and emit one `ActivityExcludedChannel` row per channel id (skip empty/None).
  - For `birthdays`, insert WITHOUT an explicit `id` so Postgres assigns the surrogate key.
  - Emit `auto_delete_type` as its enum string value.
- Print per-table row counts at the end for verification.

## Gotchas & Context
- The old SQLite still has the OLD schema (CSV column, composite birthday PK), so read it with a plain second engine / raw selects rather than the new ORM models. Write through the NEW models.
- Run with the bot OFFLINE so no new writes land mid-migration (see Phase 7). Keep the SQLite file as the rollback artifact.
- Module-level caches (starboard, activity settings) are irrelevant to the script but reinforce why a clean cutover window matters.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Script copies every table; prints per-table row counts.
- [ ] `activity_module_settings.excluded_channels` CSV is expanded into `activity_excluded_channel` rows (one per id).
- [ ] `birthdays` rows migrate with Postgres-assigned surrogate `id`s and preserved `(guild_id,user_id,name)`.
- [ ] Pure transform helpers (CSV-split, FK ordering) are unit-tested on sample inputs. (Full end-to-end copy is validated in Phase 7 against live DBs.)
- [ ] Full `pipenv run pytest` suite green on SQLite.

## References
- Plan: .docs/plans/2026-06-23-sqlite-to-supabase-postgres.md (Phase 6)
- Epic: angela-lansbury-9000-6kp

comments.author: coordinator
comments.text: Implementation complete: Phase 6: Wrote scripts/migrate_sqlite_to_postgres.py — a one-off migration copying every table from the old bumper-db.sqlite (old schema) into the new Postgres schema. READS the old side via a read-only second SQLite engine + raw SELECTs; WRITES through the corrected ORM models so snowflakes land in BigInteger columns. Copies in FK-safe order (parents before children) derived from a topological sort of FK_DEPENDENCIES. Transforms: (1) excluded_channels CSV -> one ActivityExcludedChannel row per id (empty/None skipped); (2) birthdays inserted without explicit id so Postgres assigns the surrogate key, preserving (guild_id,user_id,name,month,day,year); (3) auto_delete_type coerced to its AutoDeleteType enum value. Prints per-table row counts + total. Also resets Postgres identity sequences past copied explicit ids (dialect-guarded, no-op on SQLite; exercised in Phase 7). Factored pure helpers (split_excluded_channels, fk_safe_order/TABLE_COPY_ORDER) for DB-free unit testing. Tests: tests/test_migrate_sqlite_to_postgres.py adds 17 tests (CSV split on normal/empty/None/whitespace/single/multiple/coercion inputs; FK ordering parents-precede-children, stability, cycle detection). Full suite: 185 passed, 1 skipped (PG connectivity, expected) on SQLite. Verified: imports cleanly without a live DB, `--help` works, and `--dry-run` against the real SQLite reads all 17 tables (277,680 rows) with the CSV correctly expanding to 5 junction rows. No dead code (every function referenced from production), no test-only helpers, no swallowed errors. Files: scripts/migrate_sqlite_to_postgres.py (new), scripts/__init__.py (new), tests/test_migrate_sqlite_to_postgres.py (new). Acceptance: 5/5 criteria met.

title: Migrate bot from SQLite to Supabase Postgres
description:
## Summary
Move all bot persistence off the local `bumper-db.sqlite` file onto a free-tier Supabase Postgres, keeping the existing SQLAlchemy 2.0 + Alembic code intact. Outcome: the bot behaves identically, but every table is inspectable/editable from Supabase Studio without SSH, and data survives independently of the host. Two long-standing schema smells (`excluded_channels` CSV, `birthdays` composite PK) are cleaned up in the same pass.

## Decisions locked
1. Target is Supabase (free tier), for the Studio table-editor UI on real Postgres.
2. Free tier is sufficient; cold starts / pausing are a non-issue because the always-online bot keeps the instance warm.
3. Schema cleanups in scope: `excluded_channels` CSV -> junction table; `birthdays` -> surrogate PK.
4. Collapse the 14 SQLite-era Alembic revisions into one fresh Postgres baseline; old history discarded.
5. Dev uses local Postgres via Docker for prod parity; the test suite stays on throwaway SQLite; Supabase is production-only.

## Phase breakdown (child issues)
- Phase 1: psycopg driver, dev Postgres (Docker), connectivity
- Phase 2: Convert Discord snowflake columns to BigInteger (mandatory)
- Phase 3: Replace excluded_channels CSV with a junction table
- Phase 4: Give birthdays a surrogate primary key
- Phase 5: Generate a fresh squashed Postgres Alembic baseline
- Phase 6: SQLite -> Postgres data migration script
- Phase 7: Cutover and verification

## Sequencing
Model changes (P2/P3/P4) land and are tested on the existing SQLite harness first, where they are transparent, so the suite stays green before any Postgres exists. P1-P4 are independently committable. P5 requires P1+P2+P3+P4; P6 requires P5; P7 requires P6.

## References
- Plan: .docs/plans/2026-06-23-sqlite-to-supabase-postgres.md (and .html)
- Research: .docs/research/2026-06-23-database-backend-migration.html
close_reason: All 7 phases implemented, AI-reviewed, human-approved via crit, and committed. Phase 7 validated the migration against real prod data on local Docker Postgres. PR #15 opened for merge; Supabase production cutover documented as a manual runbook in the README.

title: Refine /starboard list pagination using a button-driven view
description:
## Summary
The initial `/starboard list` implementation renders all boards as a batch of up to 10 embeds in a single ephemeral message (`bot/cogs/starboard/starboard_commands.py:117-126`, `build_list_embeds`). Replace this with the repo's preferred pagination pattern: a `nextcord.ui.View` with ◀️/▶️ buttons that page through the boards by editing the message in place. This removes the hard 10-embed cap and matches established UX.

## Reviewer note
calebdinsmore (crit review, round 1, comment c_7a51d1): "The convention I prefer for pagination is to add pagination buttons to the message via a view and paginate that way... This is a commonly-handled user flow, so there should be abstractions we can use rather than building our own pagination view from scratch."

## Reference patterns
- This repo: `bot/cogs/image_message_delete/views/configure_prompts_view.py` (added in commit fb3e005, "Added paging to image-prompts configure") — paging via a View.
- big-poll-energy: `~/Personal/big-poll-energy/bot/cogs/polls/views/paged_poll_view.py` — `PagedPollView(nextcord.ui.View)` with `@nextcord.ui.button(emoji='◀️'/'▶️', row=4)` handlers that bump `current_index % len(items)`, call `update_buttons()`, and `interaction.response.edit_message(view=self, ...)`. Also `list_polls_view.py` in the same dir.
- IMPORTANT: prefer reusing/extracting a shared abstraction over hand-rolling another bespoke view. Caleb notes newer patterns exist in big-poll-energy that postdate Angela. Evaluate whether a generic paginator can be factored out (and applied to image-prompts too) rather than duplicating per-cog.

## Files
- `bot/cogs/starboard/starboard_commands.py` — `list` subcommand (line 117) currently sends `build_list_embeds(configs)[:10]`. Switch to instantiating the paginating view and sending its first page.
- New view file, e.g. `bot/cogs/starboard/views/starboard_list_view.py` (or a shared paginator location if extracted).
- Keep `build_list_embeds` (one embed per board) as the per-page renderer.

## Implementation Details
- View holds `configs` + `current_index`, exposes the current board's embed, ◀️/▶️ buttons wrap with modulo, disable/enable at bounds via `update_buttons()`.
- Footer/content should show `Board N/M`.
- Send ephemeral; set a sensible `timeout` and disable buttons on timeout if the pattern does so.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `/starboard list` paginates via buttons, one board per page, no 10-embed cap.
- [ ] Reuses or extracts a shared pagination abstraction rather than a fully bespoke view, per reviewer guidance.
- [ ] Tests cover: empty state, single board (buttons absent or no-op), multi-board next/prev wrap-around.
- [ ] Matches the interaction/edit pattern from the reference views above.

## References
- Plan: `.docs/plans/2026-06-22-custom-starboards.md`
- Epic: angela-lansbury-9000-17p
- Crit comment: c_7a51d1

comments.author: coordinator
comments.text: Implementation complete: Refactored /starboard list from a 10-embed batch to button-driven pagination (one board per page). Extracted a shared, reusable EmbedPaginatorView into bot/utils/views.py (◀️/▶️ modulo wrap, single-page drops nav, footer 'Board N/M', timeout disables buttons in place) — used by starboard_commands.py:129 (no dead code). Kept build_list_embeds as the per-board renderer. Tests: 94 passing (82 baseline + 12 new in tests/test_starboard_list_view.py covering empty guild, single-board no-nav, and multi-board next/prev wrap-around plus the command wiring). Acceptance: 4/4 met. Files: bot/utils/views.py (new), bot/cogs/starboard/starboard_commands.py, tests/test_starboard_list_view.py (new). Note: chose modulo wrap-around over the reference views' disable-at-bounds since the acceptance criteria require wrap-around (the two are mutually exclusive); documented in the view docstring.

title: Phase 5: Generate a fresh squashed Postgres Alembic baseline
description:
## Summary
With the corrected models in place, collapse the 14 SQLite-era Alembic revisions into a single fresh baseline generated against an empty Postgres, and apply it to create the schema.

## Files
- M `db/migrations/` — remove the 14 existing revision files
- N `db/migrations/<new>_baseline_postgres.py` — single autogenerated baseline

## Implementation Details
- With the Phase 2-4 model changes complete and `DATABASE_URL` pointed at an EMPTY Postgres (local Docker for dev work, Supabase for prod), delete the 14 existing revision files under `db/migrations/` and autogenerate one baseline. The Alembic wrapper is `Alembic(DB, 'db/migrations')` in `db/__init__.py`.
- Review the generated baseline before applying:
  - All snowflake columns are `BIGINT` (Phase 2).
  - The new `activity_excluded_channel` table is present (Phase 3).
  - `birthdays` has the surrogate `id` PK + unique constraint (Phase 4).
  - `auto_delete_type` is created as a Postgres named enum (from `Enum(AutoDeleteType)` in `auto_delete_channel_config.py:24`).
- Apply with `alembic upgrade head` against the target Postgres.

## Gotchas & Context
- Every model MUST be imported in `db/__init__.py` for Alembic autogenerate to discover it. The new `activity_excluded_channel` import from Phase 3 is required here — double-check it is present.
- Squashing is safe because the new Postgres DB starts empty; the old SQLite revision history is irrelevant to it and is intentionally discarded (decision 4).
- Generate the baseline against Postgres, not SQLite, so Postgres-specific types (BIGINT, native enum) are emitted correctly.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `db/migrations/` contains exactly one baseline revision (old 14 removed).
- [ ] `alembic upgrade head` builds the full schema on an empty Postgres with no errors.
- [ ] A test asserts the set of `DB.Model.metadata.tables` matches expectations (guards against a model not imported in `db/__init__.py`); `DB.create_all()` and the baseline produce the same tables.
- [ ] Full `pipenv run pytest` suite green on SQLite.

## References
- Plan: .docs/plans/2026-06-23-sqlite-to-supabase-postgres.md (Phase 5)
- Epic: angela-lansbury-9000-6kp

comments.text:

WHAT I DID:
1. Verified all 17 models (incl. activity_excluded_channel from Phase 3) are imported in db/__init__.py — already present, no change needed.
2. Reset the dev Postgres public schema to empty (DROP/CREATE SCHEMA public) — confirmed 0 user tables, 0 enums, no alembic_version.
3. git rm'd the 14 old revision files under db/migrations/ (kept script.py.mako scaffolding).
4. Autogenerated ONE baseline against the empty Postgres via `cli.py db revision baseline_postgres`: db/migrations/2026_06_25_d90f7e530ba0_baseline_postgres.py.
5. Applied `cli.py db upgrade` (alembic upgrade head) — succeeded with no errors.

4 SCHEMA CHECKS (verified live in Postgres after upgrade):
- BIGINT snowflakes ✓ — every snowflake column is bigint (guild_id, anchor_message, emoji_id, etc.); surrogate ids/counts/date-parts/durations stay integer.

Post-upgrade DB has 17 user tables + alembic_version (stamped d90f7e530ba0). compare_metadata(models vs live DB) reports ZERO drift — baseline exactly matches models.

TESTS: Added tests/test_baseline_schema.py asserting DB.Model.metadata.tables AND DB.create_all() each produce exactly the 17-table contract set (guards against an unimported model). db/migrations now contains EXACTLY ONE baseline revision.

ENVIRONMENT NOTE for Phase 6/7: a host-installed Postgres (user smore) is bound to loopback 127.0.0.1/::1:5432, so `localhost:5432` resolves to it (no 'postgres' role) instead of the Docker container. OrbStack serves the container on the wildcard, so I reached it via the host LAN IP (192.168.1.247:5432) — non-destructively, leaving the host PG untouched. title: Bug: single-channel /anniversary add cannot reach the modal
description:
## Summary
When a guild has exactly one registered anniversary channel, `/anniversary add` shows the channel picker with that sole option preselected (`default=True`), but there is **no way to progress to the modal**. A `nextcord.ui.StringSelect` only fires its `callback` on a *change* event; a single option that is already selected can never be changed, so `_on_select` never runs and `send_modal` is never reached. Reported in crit (r_6662a2).

## Files
- `bot/cogs/anniversary/anniversary_commands.py` (the `add` subcommand, ~lines 131-144)
- `bot/cogs/anniversary/views/channel_choice_view.py` (`ChannelChoiceView`)

## Root Cause
`ChannelChoiceView` builds a `StringSelect` whose sole `SelectOption` has `default=True` when preselected. Discord fires the select callback only when the selection changes from its current value; with one preselected option there is no possible change, so the component interaction that would call `send_modal` never occurs.

## Implementation Options (pick one; coordinate with sibling issue on Confirm/Edit buttons)
1. **Direct modal for the single-channel case (simplest):** in `add`, when `len(channels) == 1`, call `await interaction.response.send_modal(AnniversaryModal(channels[0].channel_id))` directly (a modal is a valid direct response to the slash interaction). Only fall back to `ChannelChoiceView` when `len(channels) > 1`. Apply the same single-channel shortcut to the `edit` flow's channel step.
2. **Continue button on the view:** keep the picker always-shown but add a "Continue" button whose callback opens the modal with the currently selected channel; this also dovetails with the requested Confirm/Edit preview buttons (see sibling issue) and keeps a uniform flow.

Recommendation: if the sibling "Confirm/Edit buttons" issue lands a button-driven flow, prefer option 2 for consistency; otherwise option 1 is the minimal fix.

## Gotchas & Context
- A modal MUST be the direct response to an interaction and cannot hold a select — that constraint is why the picker exists at all (see the module docstrings).
- The original plan's "always show the picker, sole channel preselected" (Decision 5 / crit c_fa6403) is what produced this dead-end; document whichever resolution is chosen so the decision trail stays coherent.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] With exactly one registered channel, `/anniversary add` reaches the modal and a submission saves.
- [ ] With two or more channels, the picker still works and routes to the chosen channel.
- [ ] The `edit` channel step has the same single-channel behavior (no dead-end).
- [ ] A regression test or manual verification note covers the single-channel path.

## References
- Crit review r_6662a2 (`~/.crit/reviews/1926c2bfb677/review.json`)
- Parent epic: angela-lansbury-9000-9d3

title: Add Confirm + Edit buttons to the /anniversary add preview
description:
## Summary
Today the `AnniversaryModal` callback persists the entry immediately and shows an ephemeral preview. The requester wants the preview to become a **confirmation step**: a **Confirm** button that saves, and an **Edit** button that jumps back into the (prefilled) modal to make changes before saving. Reported in crit (r_74fc24).

## Files
- `bot/cogs/anniversary/views/anniversary_modal.py` (`AnniversaryModal.callback`)
- `bot/cogs/anniversary/views/` (new preview/confirm view, e.g. `anniversary_preview_view.py`)
- `bot/cogs/anniversary/anniversary_utils.py` (reuse `post_embed` for the preview)

## Implementation Details
- On modal submit, **do not persist yet**. Validate (`parse_month_day`, `parse_year`), build the pending entry as a lightweight object (e.g. `SimpleNamespace` or a dict carrying title, count_label, message, month, day, year, channel_id), and render `post_embed(pending, current_year)` as an ephemeral preview attached to a new `View` with two buttons:
  - **Confirm** -> `anniversary_helper.add(...)` (or `update(...)` in the edit flow), then replace the preview with a success embed and disable the buttons.
  - **Edit** -> reopen `AnniversaryModal` prefilled from the pending values (carry channel_id through), so the user lands back in the form. Note the Discord constraint: a modal must be the direct response to the button's component interaction, which is satisfied here.
- Carry the edit-vs-add mode and the target `entry` (for edit) through the preview view so Confirm routes to the right helper call.
- Keep `title_or_default` / `label_or_default` as the rendering fallback.

## Gotchas & Context
- A modal callback can open another modal only as the direct response to a fresh component interaction (the Edit button), not by chaining off the same interaction.
- This flow likely supersedes part of the single-channel picker fix (sibling issue angela-lansbury-9000-9d3.1) — a button-driven flow is a natural place to also resolve the single-channel dead-end. Coordinate so the two land coherently (consider doing this one first, or together).
- Preview must remain ephemeral (sensitive data).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] After submitting the add modal, the user sees an ephemeral preview with **Confirm** and **Edit** buttons; nothing is persisted until Confirm.
- [ ] **Confirm** saves the entry and shows a success state (buttons disabled).
- [ ] **Edit** reopens the modal prefilled with the just-entered values; re-submitting returns to the preview.
- [ ] The edit flow (`/anniversary edit`) uses the same preview/confirm step for updates.
- [ ] Tests cover the pending-entry construction / preview embed (pure parts); manual verification note for the button round-trip.

## References
- Crit review r_74fc24 (`~/.crit/reviews/1926c2bfb677/review.json`)
- Parent epic: angela-lansbury-9000-9d3; related: angela-lansbury-9000-9d3.1

title: Modal field + copy polish (title/count-suffix required + autofilled, year placeholder, moderator wording)
description:
## Summary
A bundle of small UX/copy fixes on the anniversary modal and the add command, all from crit. Independently committable.

## Files
- `bot/cogs/anniversary/views/anniversary_modal.py`
- `bot/cogs/anniversary/anniversary_commands.py`

## Changes
1. **Title field (modal ~line 31) — crit c_276ce7.** Make it **required** and **autofill** with the literal text via `default_value='Anniversary'` instead of using placeholder-only text. (So the field arrives pre-populated with "Anniversary", which the user can overwrite.)
2. **Count field (modal ~line 46) — crit c_806073.** Rename the label from `Count word` to `Count suffix (e.g. "Nth [Suffix]")`; make it **required** and autofill `default_value='Anniversary'`. Review `max_length` (currently 100) and tighten to something sensible (e.g. 40) so the rendered "3rd <suffix>" line stays compact.
3. **Year field (modal ~line 41) — crit c_d43e1f.** Change the placeholder from `YYYY (for the count)` to `YYYY (optional)`. Keep it optional, `max_length=4`.
4. **No-channels error copy (anniversary_commands.py ~line 137) — crit c_ba13c5.** Replace "Ask a server manager" with "Ask a **moderator**" (the phrase "server manager" reads oddly).

## Gotchas & Context
- Discord modal `TextInput` supports `default_value` for autofill; a "required" field with a `default_value` arrives pre-filled and valid, which is the desired behavior.
- Because title and count-suffix are now required-and-autofilled, `title_or_default` / `label_or_default` in `anniversary_utils.py` will rarely receive blanks, but KEEP them as a safety net (they also guard the daily-post render path).
- Field count stays at 5 (still within the modal cap).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Title is required and opens pre-filled with "Anniversary" (via `default_value`).
- [ ] Count field is labeled `Count suffix (e.g. "Nth [Suffix]")`, required, pre-filled with "Anniversary", with a sensible `max_length`.
- [ ] Year placeholder reads `YYYY (optional)`.
- [ ] The no-channels error says "moderator", not "server manager".

title: anniversary_helper.update() lacks IntegrityError handling/rollback
description:
Found in Phase 3 review of angela-lansbury-9000-e87.3.

db/helpers/anniversary_helper.py update() does setattr + commit() with no try/except. The unique guard is (guild_id, user_id, title, month, day). add() catches sqlalchemy.exc.IntegrityError -> DB.s.rollback() -> returns None (the edit/add modal shows a friendly ephemeral error via 'if saved is None'). update() does not, so editing one of a user's entries to collide with another of their entries raises an uncaught IntegrityError and leaves the shared DB.s session in a failed-transaction state (no rollback), which can poison subsequent DB ops.

Fix: add a try/except sqlalchemy.exc.IntegrityError -> DB.s.rollback(); return None in update(), mirroring add(). The modal callback already guards on a None return, so no cog change is needed.

Root cause is the Phase 1 helper (angela-lansbury-9000-e87.1, closed); surfaced via the Phase 3 edit path (bot/cogs/anniversary/views/anniversary_modal.py).
close_reason: Fixed: update() now catches IntegrityError and rolls back, mirroring add(). Test added, full suite green, reviewer approved. Committed bcd012e.

No cog/modal change (the modal already guards on a None return). Added tests/test_anniversary_helper.py::test_update_collision_returns_none_and_rolls_back proving the colliding update returns None, leaves the victim row untouched, and the session stays usable (a subsequent add succeeds); confirmed the happy-path test_update_mutates_and_persists still returns the row and persists. title: Phase 7: Cutover and verification
description:
## Summary
Execute the production cutover: run the data migration with the bot offline, point production at Supabase, restart, and verify the live behavior (especially the race-sensitive starboard upsert) and the Studio admin UI.

## Files
- No new code; operational + verification work. May add a short manual smoke checklist to the README or plan.

## Implementation Details
- Stop the bot. Run the Phase 6 script (`scripts/migrate_sqlite_to_postgres.py`). Compare per-table row counts against the SQLite source.
- Set the production `DATABASE_URL` to the Supabase pooled connection string and restart the bot.
- Smoke-test the race-sensitive path: trigger a starboard reaction and confirm the `IntegrityError`-based upsert still resolves duplicates. Postgres unique-constraint violations surface as the same `sa.exc.IntegrityError` caught in `db/helpers/starboard_helper.py:160` (rolls back, then updates the existing row).
- Open Supabase Studio and confirm tables are visible and editable — the user-facing goal.

## Gotchas & Context
- Keep the SQLite file AND a Supabase backup until the bot has run cleanly for a few days.
- Confirm naive UTC datetimes read back consistently as `TIMESTAMP WITHOUT TIME ZONE`: `image_message_to_delete.delete_after` (:14), `polls.expires_at` (:21), `rolling_message_log.sent_at` (:14), `user_activity.tracking_started_on` (:16).
- Supabase free tier pauses after ~7 days of DB inactivity; the always-on bot keeps it warm, but note a keepalive ping is the fallback if a quiet period ever triggers a pause.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Migration script run; per-table row counts match the SQLite source.
- [ ] Bot runs against Supabase using only `DATABASE_URL`.
- [ ] Starboard upsert race handling verified live (duplicate reaction does not error).
- [ ] Birthdays and activity exclusions verified live.
- [ ] Tables visible/editable in Supabase Studio.
- [ ] Full `pipenv run pytest` suite green on SQLite.

## References
- Plan: .docs/plans/2026-06-23-sqlite-to-supabase-postgres.md (Phase 7)
- Epic: angela-lansbury-9000-6kp

title: Phase 3: Replace excluded_channels CSV with a junction table
description:
## Summary
Replace the `activity_module_settings.excluded_channels` comma-separated string with a proper junction table, keeping the `ActivityModuleSettingsWrapper` public API identical so callers do not change.

## Files
- N `db/model/activity_excluded_channel.py`
- M `db/model/activity_module_settings.py` — drop the `excluded_channels` column and the `excluded_channels_set` property (lines 10, 16-19)
- M `db/helpers/activity_module_settings_wrapper.py` — read/add/remove via the new table
- M `db/__init__.py` — import/register the new model (so Alembic + create_all see it)
- M `bot/cogs/activity/activity_manager.py:197-198` — stop reading raw `settings.model.excluded_channels`

## Implementation Details
- New model `ActivityExcludedChannel` (`__tablename__ = 'activity_excluded_channel'`): composite PK `(guild_id: BigInteger, channel_id: BigInteger)`. A row exists iff that channel is excluded for that guild.
- Remove `excluded_channels` column and the `excluded_channels_set` property from `ActivityModuleSettings` (`activity_module_settings.py:10,16-19`).
- Rework `ActivityModuleSettingsWrapper` (`db/helpers/activity_module_settings_wrapper.py`):
  - On init, load the excluded set from the junction table (query `ActivityExcludedChannel` by `guild_id`).
  - `excluded_channels` property returns that set (unchanged signature).
  - `add_excluded_channel(channel_id)` inserts a junction row and updates the in-memory set.
  - `remove_excluded_channel(channel_id)` deletes the junction row and updates the in-memory set.
  - Keep the public surface identical so `activity_handler.py` / `activity_commands.py` need no change.
- Fix `activity_manager.py:197-198` (currently checks `settings.model.excluded_channels` then iterates it) to use the wrapper's `excluded_channels` set instead.

## Gotchas & Context
- The wrapper is CACHED per guild in `activity_module_settings_helper.__CACHE` (returned by `get_settings`), and the cached instance holds the in-memory set. Mutations must update BOTH the junction table and the instance's set, mirroring today's behavior.
- Callers of the exclusion API:
  - `bot/events/handlers/activity_handler.py:16-17` — uses `settings.excluded_channels` (wrapper set) ✓ no change.
  - `bot/cogs/activity/activity_commands.py:100` — uses `settings.excluded_channels` ✓ no change.
  - `bot/cogs/activity/activity_manager.py:197-198` — reads raw `settings.model.excluded_channels` ✗ must change.
- `guild_id`/`channel_id` are snowflakes, so use `BigInteger` (consistent with Phase 2).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `excluded_channels` column and `excluded_channels_set` property are gone from `ActivityModuleSettings`.
- [ ] `ActivityExcludedChannel` exists, is imported in `db/__init__.py`, and is created by `DB.create_all()`.
- [ ] Wrapper API unchanged; `activity_manager.py:197-198` no longer reads the raw model field.
- [ ] Tests (new/extended, with an autouse cache-clear fixture like `test_starboard_helper.py`): empty by default; `add_excluded_channel` then read returns the id; `remove_excluded_channel` deletes it; adds are idempotent; two guilds stay isolated.
- [ ] Full `pipenv run pytest` suite green on SQLite.

## References
- Plan: .docs/plans/2026-06-23-sqlite-to-supabase-postgres.md (Phase 3)
- Epic: angela-lansbury-9000-6kp

title: Anniversary & remembrance feature
description:
## Summary
A self-contained `anniversary` cog + data layer for the angela-lansbury-9000 Discord bot (nextcord). Members record a date with an optional self-authored heading and message, route it to an admin-registered channel, and the bot posts a gentle remembrance on the day, each year, mentioning the submitter. Tone-sensitive: this is a TTC-through-parenting community and the feature must hold both joyful anniversaries and the remembrance of pregnancy/infant loss. No GIF, no "happy"; the submitter's own words carry the tone.

Grounded in good engineering, modeled on the clean **starboard** cog (pure module-level builders, thin cog, all persistence behind helpers) — explicitly NOT the messy birthday feature, which is studied only for its daily-loop mechanics and as a list of anti-patterns to avoid.

## Phases (one child issue each, sequential where noted)
- Phase 1 — Data layer: `anniversary` + `anniversary_channel` models, two helpers, Alembic migration.
- Phase 2 — Pure presentation utils: date/year parsing, ordinals, count suppression, embed builders.
- Phase 3 — Command surface: `/anniversary add|list|upcoming|edit|remove` + `/anniversary-channels add|remove|list`, modal + channel picker, autocomplete, registration. (Blocked by P1 + P2.)
- Phase 4 — Daily posting loop at 12:00 UTC with channel/member guards. (Blocked by P3.)

## Decisions locked (requester Q&A)
1. Poster configures the wording; title optional, defaults to "Anniversary".
2. Author-configurable count label; optional year drives a quiet "Nth {label}".
3. Mention the submitter (raw `<@id>` in content).
4. Post at 12:00 UTC daily.
5. Admin-registered channels (registry table); always-shown picker with sole channel preselected.
6. Feb 29 -> Feb 28 in non-leap years.
7. Both list views ephemeral: `/anniversary list` (own) and `/anniversary upcoming` (guild).
8. Entry config always editable; already-posted messages never retroactively edited (no posted-message id tracked).
9. Deregistered channel: leave entries alone; loop skips until owner re-routes via edit.
10. Skip departed submitters (verify via `guild.fetch_member`, REST; `members` intent is OFF).

## References
- Plan: `.docs/plans/2026-06-26-anniversary-remembrance-feature.md`
- Research: `.docs/research/2026-06-25-anniversary-remembrance-feature.html`
close_reason: All tasks completed successfully: Phases 1-4 (data layer, presentation utils, command surface, daily posting loop) plus follow-up bugfix 9y4 (anniversary_helper.update IntegrityError handling). All reviewer-approved, 0 blockers, suite green (307 passed, 1 skipped).

title: Phase 1: Anniversary data layer (models, helpers, migration)
description:
## Summary
Stand up the persistence layer for the anniversary feature: two new models, two new helpers, and one Alembic migration. No Discord code in this issue. Independently committable. This unblocks Phase 3 (the cog) together with Phase 2.

## Files
- `db/model/anniversary.py` (new)
- `db/model/anniversary_channel.py` (new)
- `db/helpers/anniversary_helper.py` (new)
- `db/helpers/anniversary_channel_helper.py` (new)
- `db/__init__.py` (edit: import both new models near `from db.model.starboard_entry import *`)
- `db/migrations/<rev>_add_anniversary.py` (generated)
- Tests: `tests/test_anniversary_helper.py`, `tests/test_anniversary_channel_helper.py` (new)

## Implementation Details

### Model: db/model/anniversary.py
```python
from datetime import datetime
from sqlalchemy import BigInteger, UniqueConstraint, func
from sqlalchemy.orm import Mapped, mapped_column
from db.model import DB

class Anniversary(DB.Model):
    __tablename__ = 'anniversary'
    __table_args__ = (UniqueConstraint('guild_id', 'user_id', 'title', 'month', 'day'),)

    id: Mapped[int]         = mapped_column(primary_key=True)
    guild_id: Mapped[int]   = mapped_column(BigInteger, index=True)
    user_id: Mapped[int]    = mapped_column(BigInteger, index=True)   # submitter = owner = mentioned
    channel_id: Mapped[int] = mapped_column(BigInteger)              # chosen registered channel
    title: Mapped[str]      = mapped_column(nullable=True)   # heading; render default "Anniversary"
    count_label: Mapped[str]= mapped_column(nullable=True)   # word after ordinal; default "Anniversary"
    message: Mapped[str]    = mapped_column(nullable=True)   # optional body
    month: Mapped[int]      = mapped_column()
    day: Mapped[int]        = mapped_column()
    year: Mapped[int]       = mapped_column(nullable=True)   # optional; drives Nth count
    created_at: Mapped[datetime] = mapped_column(server_default=func.now())
```

### Model: db/model/anniversary_channel.py
```python
class AnniversaryChannel(DB.Model):
    __tablename__ = 'anniversary_channel'
    __table_args__ = (UniqueConstraint('guild_id', 'channel_id'),)

    id: Mapped[int]         = mapped_column(primary_key=True)
    guild_id: Mapped[int]   = mapped_column(BigInteger, index=True)
    channel_id: Mapped[int] = mapped_column(BigInteger)
    label: Mapped[str]      = mapped_column()  # shown in the picker, e.g. "Remembrances"
```

### Helper: db/helpers/anniversary_helper.py (parameterized; NO f-string SQL)
- `add(guild_id, user_id, channel_id, title, count_label, message, month, day, year) -> Anniversary | None` — insert; catch `sqlalchemy.exc.IntegrityError` on the unique guard, `DB.s.rollback()`, return None.
- `get(id) -> Anniversary | None` — `DB.s.first(Anniversary, id=id)`.
- `list_for_user(guild_id, user_id) -> list` — `DB.s.all(...)`, order by month, day.
- `list_for_guild(guild_id) -> list`.
- `update(id, **fields) -> Anniversary | None` — fetch, `setattr` loop, commit (mirror `starboard_helper.update_config`).
- `delete(id) -> bool` — mirror `starboard_helper.remove_config`.
- `get_todays(now) -> dict[int, list]` — takes `now` as a parameter (NO `Date.now()` at module scope; testability + repo constraint). Groups rows by `guild_id`:
```python
import calendar
import sqlalchemy as sa
from db.model.anniversary import Anniversary

def get_todays(now):
    conds = [(Anniversary.month == now.month) & (Anniversary.day == now.day)]
    if now.month == 2 and now.day == 28 and not calendar.isleap(now.year):  # Decision 6
        conds.append((Anniversary.month == 2) & (Anniversary.day == 29))
    rows = DB.s.execute(sa.select(Anniversary).where(sa.or_(*conds))).scalars().all()
    out = {}
    for r in rows:
        out.setdefault(r.guild_id, []).append(r)
    return out
```
- `get_upcoming(guild_id, within_days=31) -> list` — entries whose next occurrence (this year or next, wrapping year-end) falls within the window, sorted by that next occurrence.

### Helper: db/helpers/anniversary_channel_helper.py
- `add_channel(guild_id, channel_id, label)` — catch unique-guard `IntegrityError`, roll back.
- `get_channels(guild_id)`, `get_channel(id)`, `find_by_channel_id(guild_id, channel_id)`, `remove_channel(id)`.
- No cache (registry read on slash interactions + once/day by the loop). Follow `db/helpers/starboard_helper.py` CRUD shape minus the `__CACHE`.

### Migration
Add both model imports to `db/__init__.py` FIRST (or Alembic will not see them), then:
```bash
python cli.py db revision --autogenerate -m "add anniversary + anniversary_channel"
python cli.py db upgrade
```
Review the generated file under `db/migrations/`; confirm both tables, the two unique constraints, and the indexes. Reference shape: `db/migrations/2026_06_25_*baseline_postgres.py` (uses `op.create_table(...)`).

## Gotchas & Context
- ORM is SQLAlchemy 2.0 via `sqla_wrapper`; the session is `DB.s` (`DB.s.add/.commit/.first/.all/.delete/.execute`). Models subclass `DB.Model` and import `DB` from `db.model`.
- `db/__init__.py` uses wildcard imports per model; a model not imported there is invisible to Alembic AND to `DB.create_all()` (which the test suite uses).
- `cli.py` wraps Alembic via `proper_cli`; commands are `python cli.py db revision --autogenerate -m "..."` and `python cli.py db upgrade`.
- `server_default=func.now()` works on both sqlite (tests) and Postgres (prod). No existing model uses timestamps, so this is the first; that is fine.
- Do NOT replicate birthday_helper's f-string SQL or bare `except:`.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Both models exist with the fields/constraints above and are imported in `db/__init__.py`.
- [ ] `python cli.py db revision --autogenerate` produces a migration creating both tables with unique constraints + indexes; `python cli.py db upgrade` applies cleanly.
- [ ] `anniversary_helper.add` returns a row; a second add with the same `(guild,user,title,month,day)` returns `None` (caught IntegrityError + rollback).
- [ ] `list_for_user` / `list_for_guild` scope to the right guild/user and exclude others.
- [ ] `update` mutates + persists; `delete` removes and returns False on a missing id.
- [ ] `get_todays`: matches month+day, groups by guild; a Feb 28 NON-leap call (`datetime(2025,2,28)`) ALSO returns a Feb 29 entry; a Feb 28 LEAP call (`datetime(2024,2,28)`) does NOT.
- [ ] `get_upcoming`: includes an entry a few days out, excludes one ~2 months out, wraps year-end correctly.
- [ ] Channel registry: add/get/remove work; duplicate `(guild,channel)` rejected; `find_by_channel_id` works.
- [ ] Tests in `tests/test_anniversary_helper.py` + `tests/test_anniversary_channel_helper.py` cover the above and pass (temp-sqlite + savepoint rollback per `tests/conftest.py`; no mocks).

## References
- Plan: `.docs/plans/2026-06-26-anniversary-remembrance-feature.md` (Phase 1)
- Research: `.docs/research/2026-06-25-anniversary-remembrance-feature.html`
- Parent epic: angela-lansbury-9000-e87

comments.text: Implementation complete: Phase 1 anniversary data layer complete (persistence only, no Discord code). Added models db/model/anniversary.py + anniversary_channel.py with their unique constraints and indexes, registered both in db/__init__.py near starboard. Helpers db/helpers/anniversary_helper.py (add/get/list_for_user/list_for_guild/update/delete/get_todays/get_upcoming) and anniversary_channel_helper.py (add_channel/get_channels/get_channel/find_by_channel_id/remove_channel) — all parameterized SQLAlchemy, narrow IntegrityError handling, NO f-string SQL, NO bare except. get_todays(now) takes now as a param (no module-scope clock) and pulls Feb 29 entries on a non-leap Feb 28; get_upcoming defaults now inside the function body and wraps year-end. Generated + applied Alembic migration 2026_06_26_1bfa356ecc71_add_anniversary_anniversary_channel (db upgrade clean, db current at head; reviewed file confirms both tables, both UniqueConstraints, guild_id/user_id indexes). Updated the EXPECTED_TABLES contract in tests/test_baseline_schema.py for the two new tables. Tests: 23 new (test_anniversary_helper.py 16 + test_anniversary_channel_helper.py 7), no mocks, temp-sqlite + savepoint per conftest. Full suite green: 208 passed, 1 skipped. Acceptance: 9/9 criteria met. title: Phase 2: Anniversary presentation utils (parsing, ordinals, embeds)
description:
## Summary
All the testable presentation logic for the anniversary feature as pure functions, with zero Discord interaction objects. Independently committable and independent of Phase 1 (operates on duck-typed entry objects; tests use fakes). Together with Phase 1 this unblocks Phase 3.

## Files
- `bot/cogs/anniversary/__init__.py` (new; package init)
- `bot/cogs/anniversary/anniversary_utils.py` (new)
- Tests: `tests/test_anniversary_utils.py` (new)

def title_or_default(title): return (title or '').strip() or 'Anniversary'

## Gotchas & Context
- Embed color constants and the `info/error/success` builders live in `bot/utils/messages.py` (`INFO_COLOR = 3700200`). Import `messages` from `bot.utils`.

title: Phase 3: Anniversary command surface (cog, modal, channel picker)
description:
## Summary
The interactive surface: a new `AnniversaryCommands` cog with two slash groups, a shared add/edit modal, and a channel-picker view. Registered in the bot. Blocked by Phase 1 (models/helpers) and Phase 2 (utils). Independently committable once those land.

## Files
- `bot/cogs/anniversary/anniversary_commands.py` (new; cog + slash groups + autocomplete)
- `bot/cogs/anniversary/views/anniversary_modal.py` (new; shared add/edit modal)
- `bot/cogs/anniversary/views/channel_choice_view.py` (new; channel select that opens the modal)
- `bot/cogs/__init__.py` (edit: add `AnniversaryCommands` to `__all__` + import)
- `bot/app.py` (edit: import + `bot.add_cog(AnniversaryCommands(bot))` in `run()`, alongside the others ~line 69)
- Tests: `tests/test_anniversary_commands.py` (new)

## Implementation Details

### Slash groups (starboard pattern)
- `/anniversary` — no permission gate, `force_global=True`.
- `/anniversary-channels` — `default_member_permissions=Permissions(manage_guild=True)`, `force_global=True`.
Subcommands via `@group.subcommand(...)`. Replies: `interaction.send(embed=..., ephemeral=True)` with `messages.error/success/info`.

### Command map
| command | gate | flow |
|---|---|---|
| `/anniversary add` | everyone | reject if no channels -> channel picker (sole preselected) -> modal -> save -> ephemeral preview |
| `/anniversary list` | everyone | caller's own entries, ephemeral, paginated |
| `/anniversary upcoming` | everyone | guild next ~31 days, ephemeral, paginated |
| `/anniversary edit` | owner/manager | required `entry` autocomplete -> channel picker (current preselected) -> prefilled modal -> update |
| `/anniversary remove` | owner/manager | `entry` autocomplete -> delete -> ephemeral confirm |
| `/anniversary-channels add` | manage_guild | channel + label; perms preflight; save |
| `/anniversary-channels remove` | manage_guild | `channel` autocomplete -> delete from registry |
| `/anniversary-channels list` | manage_guild | ephemeral list of registered channels |

### Add flow (Discord: a modal MUST be the direct response to an interaction)
`/anniversary add` -> if `anniversary_channel_helper.get_channels(guild_id)` is empty, reject (ephemeral `messages.error`). Else `interaction.send(view=ChannelChoiceView(channels), ephemeral=True)`. The select's COMPONENT interaction calls `interaction.response.send_modal(AnniversaryModal(channel_id))`. The picker always shows; when exactly one channel is registered it is preselected (Decision 5).

### Modal (<= 5 TextInputs; mirror bot/cogs/polls/views/create_poll_modal.py)
```python
class AnniversaryModal(nextcord.ui.Modal):
    def __init__(self, channel_id, *, entry=None):
        super().__init__(title='Add an anniversary' if entry is None else 'Edit anniversary')
        self.channel_id = channel_id   # chosen in the picker (add) or re-confirmed there (edit)
        self.entry = entry
        # fields, prefilled from entry when editing (default_value=...):
        #   title      (optional, short)        placeholder "Anniversary"
        #   date       (required)               placeholder "MM/DD"
        #   year       (optional)               placeholder "YYYY (for the count)"
        #   count word (optional)               placeholder "Anniversary"
        #   message    (optional, paragraph)    style=nextcord.TextInputStyle.paragraph
        # exactly 5 fields = Discord modal max

    async def callback(self, interaction):
        try:
            month, day = anniversary_utils.parse_month_day(self.date.value)
            year = anniversary_utils.parse_year(self.year.value)
        except ValueError as e:
            return await interaction.response.send_message(
                embed=messages.error(str(e)), ephemeral=True)   # cannot reopen the modal
        # entry is None -> anniversary_helper.add(...); else anniversary_helper.update(
        #   self.entry.id, title=..., count_label=..., message=..., month=month, day=day,
        #   year=year, channel_id=self.channel_id)
        # then ephemeral post_embed(...) preview
```

### Edit reuses the add flow (Decision 9)
`/anniversary edit` takes a REQUIRED `entry` autocomplete option (resolve + authorize first via `_can_manage`; 404/permission -> ephemeral error). Then mirror add exactly: the same `ChannelChoiceView`, preselected to the entry's CURRENT channel, then the same `AnniversaryModal(channel_id, entry=entry)` prefilled from the entry. Keeping the channel is one confirming tap; re-routing (after a channel was deregistered) is just picking a different option. No separate optional `channel` slash option.

### Authorization & autocomplete (static helpers on the cog, like starboard)
```python
@staticmethod
def _can_manage(interaction, entry):
    return entry is not None and entry.guild_id == interaction.guild_id and (
        entry.user_id == interaction.user.id
        or interaction.user.guild_permissions.manage_guild)

# _entry_choices(interaction, focused): caller's entries, or ALL guild entries when the caller
#   has manage_guild; returns {label: str(id)}, capped at 25; substring filter on focused.
# _channel_choices(interaction, focused): {label: str(channel_id)} from the registry
#   (used by /anniversary-channels remove autocomplete).
```
Autocomplete via `@subcmd.on_autocomplete('entry')` -> `interaction.response.send_autocomplete({label: value})` (starboard pattern). `/anniversary-channels add` mirrors starboard's `_perms_warning`: WARN (do not block) when the bot lacks Send Messages / Embed Links in the target channel.

### list / upcoming
Both ephemeral, paginated via `EmbedPaginatorView(build_list_embeds(...), noun='Anniversary').send(interaction)`. `list` = `anniversary_helper.list_for_user(guild_id, user_id)`; `upcoming` = `anniversary_helper.get_upcoming(guild_id)`. Show an ephemeral info embed when empty.

## Gotchas & Context
- A modal cannot hold a select -> the channel choice is a separate `View` (add) or the reused picker (edit).
- A modal cannot be reopened on a validation error -> the callback replies ephemeral and asks to re-run; keep parsing forgiving.
- Modal field cap is 5; the five fields above are exactly at the limit.
- `EmbedPaginatorView` lives in `bot/utils/views.py`; `.send(interaction)` defaults to ephemeral and strips the buttons when there is a single page.
- Cog registration: `bot/cogs/__init__.py` has an `__all__` list + imports; `bot/app.py` `run()` calls `bot.add_cog(...)` for each. Add `AnniversaryCommands` to both. The cog `__init__(self, bot)` stores `self.bot` (the loop start lands in Phase 4).
- `interaction.user.guild_permissions.manage_guild` gives the manager check.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `/anniversary add` rejects when no channel is registered; otherwise shows a channel picker (sole channel preselected) that opens the modal; a valid submission persists and previews ephemerally.
- [ ] Title, year, count-word, message are all optional; blank title renders "Anniversary"; a bad date yields a friendly ephemeral error (no crash).
- [ ] `/anniversary list` (own) and `/anniversary upcoming` (guild) are ephemeral and paginate via `EmbedPaginatorView`.
- [ ] `/anniversary edit` resolves a required `entry`, reuses the channel picker (current preselected) + prefilled modal, and can re-route the channel; `/anniversary remove` deletes. Owners reach only their own entries; `manage_guild` members reach any entry in the guild.
- [ ] `/anniversary-channels add|remove|list` are `manage_guild`-gated; `add` warns when the bot lacks Send Messages / Embed Links.
- [ ] `AnniversaryCommands` is exported from `bot/cogs/__init__.py` and added in `bot/app.py` `run()`.
- [ ] Tests in `tests/test_anniversary_commands.py`: `_can_manage` (owner/manager yes, unrelated no, wrong guild no); `_entry_choices`/`_channel_choices` shape `{label: str(id)}`, owner-vs-manager scoping, 25-cap, substring filter (use `SimpleNamespace` fakes); cog import smoke. Tests pass.

## References
- Plan: `.docs/plans/2026-06-26-anniversary-remembrance-feature.md` (Phase 3)
- Reference cog: `bot/cogs/starboard/starboard_commands.py`; modal: `bot/cogs/polls/views/create_poll_modal.py`; pagination: `bot/utils/views.py`
- Parent epic: angela-lansbury-9000-e87; blocked by angela-lansbury-9000-e87.1 and angela-lansbury-9000-e87.2
