Built the AnniversaryCommands cog with two slash groups: /anniversary (force_global, open) with add/list/upcoming/edit/remove, and /anniversary-channels (manage_guild-gated) with add/remove/list, plus entry & channel autocomplete. Added the shared AnniversaryModal (exactly 5 TextInputs: title/date/year/count-word/message, prefilled on edit) and ChannelChoiceView (StringSelect that opens the modal, sole/current channel preselected) — split this way because a Discord modal must be the direct interaction response and cannot hold a select. Add rejects when no channel is registered; bad dates surface a friendly ephemeral error (modal can't reopen); edit reuses the add flow (same picker+modal) and can re-route the channel; owners reach only their own entries, manage_guild reaches any (_can_manage). Registered the cog in bot/cogs/__init__.py (__all__ + import) and bot/app.py run(). No daily loop (that's Phase 4). Files: bot/cogs/anniversary/anniversary_commands.py (new), bot/cogs/anniversary/views/{__init__,anniversary_modal,channel_choice_view}.py (new), bot/cogs/__init__.py + bot/app.py (edited), tests/test_anniversary_commands.py (new). Tests: 294 passing (21 new covering _can_manage owner/manager/unrelated/wrong-guild, _entry_choices/_channel_choices shape+scoping+25-cap+substring filter, resolvers, cog-export smoke), 1 pre-existing skip. Acceptance: 7/7 criteria met. No dead code, no bare excepts, no f-string SQL.

title: Phase 4: Anniversary daily posting loop
description:
## Summary
Add the daily posting loop to the anniversary cog: one `@tasks.loop` at 12:00 UTC that posts a neutral embed per today's entry to its own channel, mentions the submitter, and skips entries whose channel was deregistered or whose submitter has left the guild. Blocked by Phase 3 (edits the cog file + adds a pure helper to anniversary_utils). Independently committable once Phase 3 lands.

## Files
- `bot/cogs/anniversary/anniversary_commands.py` (edit: add the loop, the `.error` handler, `_fetch_member_or_none`, and `self.post_anniversaries.start()` in `__init__`)
- `bot/cogs/anniversary/anniversary_utils.py` (edit: add pure `partition_postable(entries, registered_ids, is_member)`)
- Tests: extend `tests/test_anniversary_utils.py` (or a new `tests/test_anniversary_loop.py`)

## Implementation Details
Model after `bot/cogs/birthday/birthday_commands.py:52-149`, but CORRECTED (real daily schedule, parameterized data access, narrow excepts).
```python
def __init__(self, bot):
    self.bot = bot
    self.post_anniversaries.start()

@tasks.loop(time=time(hour=12, minute=0, second=0, tzinfo=timezone.utc))  # Decision 4
async def post_anniversaries(self):
    if not self.bot.is_ready():
        return
    today = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
    for guild_id, entries in anniversary_helper.get_todays(today).items():
        try:
            guild = self.bot.get_guild(guild_id)
            if guild is None:
                continue
            registered = {c.channel_id for c in anniversary_channel_helper.get_channels(guild_id)}
            for entry in entries:
                if entry.channel_id not in registered:
                    continue                                  # Decision 9: deregistered -> skip
                member = await _fetch_member_or_none(guild, entry.user_id)
                if member is None:
                    continue                                  # Decision 10: submitter gone -> skip
                channel = await bot_utils.get_or_fetch_channel(guild, entry.channel_id)
                if channel is None:
                    continue
                embed = anniversary_utils.post_embed(entry, today.year)
                await channel.send(content=f'<@{entry.user_id}>', embed=embed,
                                   allowed_mentions=nextcord.AllowedMentions(users=True))
        except nextcord.Forbidden as e:
            sentry_sdk.capture_exception(e)
        except Exception as e:
            sentry_sdk.capture_exception(e)

@post_anniversaries.error
async def post_anniversaries_error(self, e):
    sentry_sdk.capture_exception(e)
    await asyncio.sleep(60)
    self.post_anniversaries.restart()
```
`_fetch_member_or_none(guild, user_id)`:
```python
async def _fetch_member_or_none(guild, user_id):
    try:
        return await guild.fetch_member(user_id)
    except nextcord.NotFound:
        return None
```

### Testability seam (pure)
Extract the skip rules into `anniversary_utils.partition_postable(entries, registered_ids, is_member) -> (postable, skipped)` where `is_member` is a callable `user_id -> bool`. The loop may call it or mirror it; the network calls (fetch_member, channel.send) stay thin.

## Gotchas & Context
- The `members` intent is OFF (`bot/app.py:18`), so the member cache is empty. Use `guild.fetch_member` (a REST call that works without the intent and raises `nextcord.NotFound` when the user has left). Do NOT use `guild.get_member` for the membership guard.
- The raw `<@user_id>` mention renders and pings without the members intent; set `AllowedMentions(users=True)` so the ping actually fires.
- `bot_utils.get_or_fetch_channel(guild, channel_id)` is the existing helper the birthday loop uses to resolve a channel.
- `now`/`current_year` flow in as parameters (`datetime.now(timezone.utc)` passed at the call site); helpers/utils never call `Date.now()` at module scope (repo constraint).
- CRITICAL: the loop decorator MUST use `time=time(hour=12, ...)`, never a `seconds=` interval. The birthday feature shipped a `@tasks.loop(seconds=15)` test loop by accident; do not repeat it.
- One failing entry/guild must never abort the loop (per-guild try/except; continue on per-entry skips).
- Imports needed at top of the cog module: `asyncio`, `from datetime import datetime, timezone, time`, `nextcord`, `sentry_sdk`, `from nextcord.ext import tasks`, the two helpers, `anniversary_utils`, `bot_utils`.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] The loop is scheduled with `time=time(hour=12, minute=0, second=0, tzinfo=timezone.utc)` (no `seconds=` interval) and is started in `__init__`.
- [ ] It posts one neutral embed per today's entry to that entry's own `channel_id`, with `content='<@user_id>'` and `AllowedMentions(users=True)`; no gif, no "happy".
- [ ] Entries are skipped (not posted) when their `channel_id` is not in the guild's registry, or when `fetch_member` returns None (submitter left).
- [ ] A `nextcord.Forbidden` or any exception on one guild/entry is captured to Sentry and does not abort the rest of the loop; `.error` handler sleeps 60s and restarts.
- [ ] Feb 29 entries post on Feb 28 in non-leap years (via Phase 1 `get_todays`).
- [ ] `partition_postable` is pure and unit-tested: registered channel + present member -> postable; deregistered channel -> skipped; absent member -> skipped.
- [ ] A `post_embed` integration check: a today entry WITH a year shows "{ordinal} {label} · {Month Day}" in the footer; WITHOUT a year shows just the date.
- [ ] Tests pass.

## References
- Plan: `.docs/plans/2026-06-26-anniversary-remembrance-feature.md` (Phase 4)
- Reference (structure only, not its bugs): `bot/cogs/birthday/birthday_commands.py:52-149`
- Parent epic: angela-lansbury-9000-e87; blocked by angela-lansbury-9000-e87.3

title: Phase 1: psycopg driver, dev Postgres (Docker), connectivity
description:
## Summary
Prepare the codebase to talk to Postgres without changing any schema yet: add the psycopg driver, stand up a local Postgres for development via Docker (prod parity), and document the `DATABASE_URL` workflow. No model or query changes in this phase.

## Files
- M `Pipfile` — add psycopg driver
- N `docker-compose.yml` — local Postgres service for dev
- N `.env.example` — local Docker + prod (Supabase) `DATABASE_URL` forms
- M `README.md` (DB section) — document the dev DB setup

## Implementation Details
- Add `psycopg = {extras = ["binary"], version = "*"}` (psycopg 3) to `[packages]` in the Pipfile; run `pipenv lock`.
- No application code changes: `db/model/__init__.py:5` already reads `DATABASE_URL` with a SQLite default (`DB = SQLAlchemy(os.environ.get('DATABASE_URL', 'sqlite:///bumper-db.sqlite'))`), so the engine is selected purely by env var.
- Dev DB (Docker): add `docker-compose.yml` with a single `postgres` service pinned to the same major version Supabase runs (Postgres 15), exposing `5432`, with a named volume for persistence. Local URL form: `postgresql+psycopg://postgres:postgres@localhost:5432/bumper`. Daily dev loop: `docker compose up -d` then run the bot.
- Prod (Supabase) is a manual owner step: create the project, grab the POOLED connection string, URL form `postgresql+psycopg://USER:PASS@HOST:PORT/postgres`.
- `.env.example`: show the local-Docker URL as the dev default and the Supabase URL commented out. Document the workflow in the README DB section (which already explains the env-var contract).

## Gotchas & Context
- psycopg is a SYNCHRONOUS driver, so the shared `DB.s` session and every helper in `db/helpers/` keep working unchanged. Do NOT introduce async.
- Pin the Docker Postgres major version to match Supabase so the local schema matches what prod will build.
- For Supabase, use the pooled (PgBouncer) port; the single long-running bot process needs only a few connections, so transaction-mode pooling is fine.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `pipenv install` resolves with psycopg present; `Pipfile.lock` updated.
- [ ] `docker compose up -d` starts a Postgres 15 instance reachable at `localhost:5432`.
- [ ] Setting `DATABASE_URL` to the local Docker URL lets the bot connect (manual check) without code changes.
- [ ] `.env.example` documents both local and Supabase URL forms; README DB section updated.
- [ ] Added connectivity smoke test reads `TEST_PG_URL`; if set, opens an engine and runs `SELECT 1`; skipped otherwise. Default `pipenv run pytest` suite stays on SQLite and remains green.

## References
- Plan: .docs/plans/2026-06-23-sqlite-to-supabase-postgres.md (Phase 1)
- Research: .docs/research/2026-06-23-database-backend-migration.html
- Epic: angela-lansbury-9000-6kp

Changes:

- docker-compose.yml (new): single `postgres` service pinned to Postgres 15 (Supabase parity), exposes 5432, named `postgres-data` volume for persistence, pg_isready healthcheck. DB `bumper`, user/pass postgres.
- .env.example (new): dev default DATABASE_URL = postgresql+psycopg://postgres:postgres@localhost:5432/bumper; Supabase pooled prod form commented out; BOT_TOKEN documented.
- README.md: new Database section documenting the env-var contract, `docker compose up -d` dev loop, Supabase pooled connection note, and the TEST_PG_URL parity-check usage.
- tests/test_pg_connectivity.py (new): reads TEST_PG_URL; opens its own engine and runs SELECT 1 if set, otherwise pytest.mark.skipif skips. Uses sqlalchemy create_engine directly (not the SQLite-bound DB singleton).

No app code changes needed — db/model/__init__.py already selects the engine purely from DATABASE_URL. No async introduced.

Acceptance: 5/5 criteria addressed (2 require Docker/live Postgres which are manual owner steps — Docker CLI not installed in this env).
Tests: `pipenv run pytest` = 110 passed, 1 skipped (connectivity test skips cleanly without TEST_PG_URL). Suite stays on SQLite and is green.

title: Custom Starboards: review follow-ups
description: Follow-up work from the post-implementation crit review of the Custom Starboards feature (branch: starboards). Separate from the planned-work follow-ups epic (angela-lansbury-9000-e17). Parent feature epic: angela-lansbury-9000-17p.

title: Remove the starboard name field
description:
## Summary
Remove the optional `name` field from starboard configs. Now that duplicate
channel+emoji boards are prevented (`find_duplicate_config`), every board is
uniquely and meaningfully identified by `#channel | emoji`, and channels usually
carry a descriptive name already. The `name` field is therefore redundant surface
area. Decided during the post-implementation crit review (comment c_e66cf1).

## Migration approach (IMPORTANT — active development, do NOT stack a new migration)
The starboard tables migration has not shipped anywhere, so regenerate it in place
rather than adding a column-drop migration on top:

1. `pipenv run python cli.py db downgrade` — roll back the starboard-tables
   migration (`db/migrations/2026_06_23_44433ddac1b1_add_starboard_tables.py`).
2. Delete that migration file.
3. Make the model change (remove `name` from `StarboardConfig`).
4. `pipenv run python cli.py db revision "add starboard tables"` — autogenerate a
   fresh migration that creates the tables WITHOUT the `name` column. Confirm
   every model is still imported in `db/__init__.py` so autogenerate sees them.
5. `pipenv run python cli.py db upgrade` and verify the schema.

## Files
- `db/model/starboard_config.py` — remove the `name: Mapped[...]` column.
- `db/migrations/2026_06_23_44433ddac1b1_add_starboard_tables.py` — deleted and
  regenerated (new revision id/filename).
- `db/helpers/starboard_helper.py` — drop the `name` param from `add_config` (and
  any other reference). `find_duplicate_config` is unaffected.
- `bot/cogs/starboard/starboard_commands.py`:
  - `add`: remove the `name` SlashOption.
  - `edit`: remove the `name` SlashOption and its `updates['name']` handling.
  - `board_summary`: drop the `(Name) ` prefix; format becomes
    `#channel | emoji | **≥ N**` (keep the trailing `| disabled` for disabled
    boards). Update docstring.
  - `board_label` / `build_list_embeds` follow from `board_summary` automatically.
- `README.md` — update if it documents the `name` option.

## Tests to update
- `tests/test_starboard_commands.py`:
  - `_config` factory: drop the `name` kwarg.
  - `test_board_summary_standard_format_with_name`, `test_board_summary_omits_name_when_absent`,
    `test_board_label_includes_name_when_set` — remove/rework to the no-name format.
  - `test_build_list_embeds_single_embed` — line now `1. <#10> | ⭐ | **≥ 5**`.
  - `test_add_config_persists_and_invalidates_cache` — drop `name`.
- `tests/test_starboard_helper.py` — drop `name=` from any `add_config` calls.
- `tests/test_starboard_list_view.py` — `_add` helper passes `name=`; remove it.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `name` removed from model, helper, both commands, and all rendering.
- [ ] Starboard-tables migration regenerated in place (no separate drop migration);
      `db upgrade` from clean produces a `starboard_config` table with no `name`.
- [ ] `board_summary` renders `#channel | emoji | **≥ N**` (+ `| disabled`).
- [ ] Full test suite green.

## References
- Plan: `.docs/plans/2026-06-22-custom-starboards.md`
- Feature epic: angela-lansbury-9000-17p
- Crit comment: c_e66cf1

title: Phase 4: Give birthdays a surrogate primary key
description:
## Summary
Replace the `birthdays` table's `(guild_id, user_id, name)` composite primary key (which puts a free-text `name` in the PK) with a surrogate autoincrement `id` plus a unique constraint that preserves the no-duplicate guarantee.

## Files
- M `db/model/birthday.py`
- M `db/helpers/birthday_helper.py` — verify queries remain valid (likely no change)

## Implementation Details
- In `db/model/birthday.py`, replace the composite PK `(guild_id, user_id, name)` with:
  - `id: Mapped[int] = mapped_column(primary_key=True)` (autoincrement surrogate)
  - `guild_id` / `user_id` as `BigInteger` (per Phase 2), no longer `primary_key`
  - `name` as a normal column, no longer `primary_key`
  - Add `__table_args__ = (UniqueConstraint('guild_id', 'user_id', 'name'),)` to keep duplicates out.
- `add_birthday` (`birthday_helper.py:14-21`) relies on catching `sa.exc.IntegrityError` for duplicate prevention; the new unique constraint raises the same error, so the function is unchanged.
- `delete_birthday` deletes by `(guild_id, user_id, name)` (`birthday_helper.py`) — still valid against the unique tuple, no change.

## Gotchas & Context
- `birthday_helper.py` issues several RAW f-string SQL queries via `sa.text(f"SELECT * FROM birthdays WHERE ...")` (around lines 50, 75-95, 120) interpolating integer date parts and `guild_id`. These are standard SQL and run on Postgres unchanged; after migration, verify `SELECT *` rows still expose `.guild_id`, `.user_id`, `.name`, `.month`, `.day`, `.year`. Interpolated values are ints from `datetime`, so not an injection concern. Parametrizing these is a DEFERRED future cleanup, explicitly NOT in scope for this migration.
- `guild_id`/`user_id` are snowflakes -> `BigInteger`.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `birthdays` has a surrogate `id` PK and a `UniqueConstraint('guild_id','user_id','name')`.
- [ ] New `tests/test_birthday_helper.py`: adding the same `(guild, user, name)` twice returns `False` (constraint holds); different names for one user coexist; delete-by-name removes exactly one row; `list_birthdays` returns a user's rows.
- [ ] Full `pipenv run pytest` suite green on SQLite.

## References
- Plan: .docs/plans/2026-06-23-sqlite-to-supabase-postgres.md (Phase 4)
- Epic: angela-lansbury-9000-6kp

Replaced composite PK (guild_id, user_id, name) in db/model/birthday.py with an autoincrement surrogate `id` PK; guild_id/user_id kept as BigInteger (non-PK), name as a normal column, and added `__table_args__ = (UniqueConstraint('guild_id','user_id','name'),)` to preserve the no-duplicate guarantee. birthday_helper.py needed NO changes — add_birthday still catches IntegrityError (now from the unique constraint), delete_birthday deletes by the unique tuple, and raw f-string SELECT queries are untouched (out of scope). Added tests/test_birthday_helper.py covering: duplicate (guild,user,name) returns False, different names for one user coexist, delete-by-name removes exactly one row, and list_birthdays returns only a user's rows. Tests: 4 new passing; full `pipenv run pytest` suite green (166 passed, 1 skipped) on SQLite. Acceptance: 3/3 criteria met. Files changed: db/model/birthday.py, tests/test_birthday_helper.py.

title: Phase 2: Convert Discord snowflake columns to BigInteger
description:
## Summary
Convert every Discord-snowflake column across all models from a bare `Mapped[int]` to an explicit `BigInteger`. This is the one MANDATORY code change for the migration: on SQLite bare ints store 64-bit values fine, but on Postgres a bare `int` maps to 32-bit `INTEGER`, which overflows on Discord snowflakes (~1e18). Transparent on SQLite, so the existing suite stays green.

## Files (all of db/model/ with snowflake columns)
- M `db/model/user_settings.py` — guild_id, user_id (composite PK)
- M `db/model/image_message_to_delete.py` — guild_id, channel_id, message_id, author_id
- M `db/model/activity_role.py` — guild_id, role_id
- M `db/model/guild_config.py` — guild_id, birthday_channel_id, baby_month_milestone_channel_id
- M `db/model/starboard_entry.py` — guild_id, original_message_id, original_channel_id, posted_message_id, author_id
- M `db/model/starboard_config.py` — guild_id, target_channel_id, emoji_id
- M `db/model/user_channel_settings.py` — guild_id, channel_id, user_id (composite PK)
- M `db/model/auto_delete_channel_config.py` — guild_id, channel_id, anchor_message, original_anchor_message
- M `db/model/activity_module_settings.py` — guild_id, inactive_role_id, break_role_id, log_channel
- M `db/model/santa_participant.py` — santa_id, recipient_id
- M `db/model/birthday.py` — guild_id, user_id
- M `db/model/polls.py` — poll_question: message_id, guild_id, channel_id, author_id; poll_response: respondent_id
- M `db/model/user_activity.py` — guild_id, user_id (composite PK)
- M `db/model/rolling_message_log.py` — message_id, guild_id, author_id

## Implementation Details
Pattern:
```python
from sqlalchemy import BigInteger
# before
guild_id: Mapped[int] = mapped_column(primary_key=True)
# after
guild_id: Mapped[int] = mapped_column(BigInteger, primary_key=True)
```
- In `auto_delete_channel_config.py:25-27`, the columns currently use explicit `Integer()` (still 32-bit). Replace with `BigInteger` on `anchor_message` and `original_anchor_message`. KEEP `delete_after_minutes` as `Integer()` — it is a duration, not a snowflake.
- Leave plain `int`: surrogate keys (`id` autoincrement on starboard_entry/starboard_config/poll_question/poll_choice), counts (`star_count`, `threshold`), date parts (`month`/`day`/`year` on birthday), durations (`delete_after_minutes`, `user_channel_settings.delete_after`, `rolling_month_window`, `grace_period_months`, activity_role `min`/`max`).
- Full per-column inventory is in the research doc, section 03.

## Gotchas & Context
- Discord snowflakes fit Postgres signed `BIGINT` (max ~9.2e18; current snowflakes ~1e18). No unsigned needed.
- On SQLite this change is a runtime no-op, so the existing suite stays green. The effect is only visible once the schema is created on Postgres (Phase 5) — which is exactly why this lands and is verified first.
- Do not change `activity_module_settings.excluded_channels` here — it is a CSV string handled in Phase 3.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Every snowflake column listed above is declared with `BigInteger`.
- [ ] New `tests/test_model_column_types.py` asserts, via `Model.__table__.c.<col>.type`, that each snowflake column is a `BigInteger` instance.
- [ ] A round-trip test inserts a realistic snowflake (e.g. `112233445566778899`) into a representative model and reads it back unchanged.
- [ ] Full `pipenv run pytest` suite green on SQLite.

## References
- Plan: .docs/plans/2026-06-23-sqlite-to-supabase-postgres.md (Phase 2)
- Research: .docs/research/2026-06-23-database-backend-migration.html (section 03 inventory)
- Epic: angela-lansbury-9000-6kp

Surrogate id keys, counts (star_count/threshold), date parts (month/day/year), and durations correctly left as plain int.
